subject: An ornate curved scimitar with a long, sweeping blade and a hilt styled with feline / leonine motifs, displayed against a plain dark background as a museum-style object portrait
description: A single curved scimitar floats centered against a flat, near-black background. The blade is long, slim, and dramatically curved with a polished metallic surface that catches a soft highlight along its edge. The hilt and pommel are sculpted with organic, animalistic flourishes evoking a lion or other big cat — curling forms and tooth- or claw-like accents in pale bone-white and silver tones. There are no figures, no environment, no scene — just the weapon presented as an isolated artifact study. Lighting is soft and directional, picking out the sweep of the blade and the carved detail of the grip.
background: flat dark void, near-black with subtle gradient
